title: carex sororia var. involucrata
intro: Carex sororia var. involucrata, also known as the involucrate var. of the woodland sedge, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the genus Carex. It is native to North America and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional herbal medicine. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce swelling, soothe skin irritations, and aid in the treatment of wounds. Carex sororia var. involucrata can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to promote wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues.
